description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged and weathered surface, featuring a mix of gray, light brown, and bluish-green hues. The rock's surface is uneven, with deep crevices, jagged edges, and a few smoother patches, suggesting natural erosion and exposure to the elements. One side of the rock displays a more prominent bluish tint, possibly due to mineral deposits or moisture. The overall form is asymmetrical, with one flatter, broader face and a more angular, pointed end. A small, yellowish twig or plant fragment is visible resting on the upper surface, adding a touch of organic detail. The rock appears dense and solid, typical of stones used as natural barriers or landscaping features in urban parks or roadside environments.
description: A rugged, irregularly shaped rock with gray, brown, and bluish-green tones, featuring deep crevices and a weathered surface. It appears dense and solid, suitable as a natural barrier or landscaping element.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest, flattest face of the rock, highlighting its rough texture and subtle color variations in gray and brown.
description_view_1: This angle reveals the rock's more angular, pointed end and a visible yellowish twig on top, with a slight greenish tint on the right side.
description_view_2: The rock's most prominent bluish-green surface is visible here, with deep crevices and a rugged, uneven profile.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the rock's vertical height and angularity, with a mix of gray and greenish hues and pronounced surface irregularities.
attribute_location: urban park or roadside landscaping area
